title: Distributive Property and Area
essential_question: How can you use the distributive property to find the area of a rectangle?

learn.title: What Is the Distributive Property?
learn.content: The DISTRIBUTIVE PROPERTY says: a × (b + c) = (a × b) + (a × c). It lets you SPLIT one of the numbers into two parts, multiply each part, and ADD the results.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.11 Lesson 11.6 'Distributive Property and Area'
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 4 Topic D — Distributive Property
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Why Use It?
learn.content: Sometimes 6 × 8 is hard to recall. Split: 6 × 8 = 6 × (5 + 3) = 30 + 18 = 48. Splitting into facts you KNOW makes hard multiplication easier.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.11 Lesson 11.6 'Distributive Property and Area'
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 4 Topic D — Distributive Property
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Area Picture of Distributive Property
learn.content: Imagine a 6 × 8 rectangle. Cut it into a 6 × 5 rectangle and a 6 × 3 rectangle. Total area = (6×5) + (6×3) = 30 + 18 = 48 sq units. Same as the original.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.11 Lesson 11.6 'Distributive Property and Area'
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 4 Topic D — Distributive Property
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Choose Smart Splits
learn.content: Pick splits that use facts you know well. Common choices: 5 + something. Example: 7 × 9 = 7 × (5 + 4) = 35 + 28 = 63.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.11 Lesson 11.6 'Distributive Property and Area'
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 4 Topic D — Distributive Property
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Multiple Ways to Split
learn.content: There are MANY ways to split. 8 × 7 = 8 × (5+2) = 40+16 = 56. Or 8 × 7 = 8 × (4+3) = 32+24 = 56. Or 8 × (6+1) = 48+8 = 56. All give the same answer.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.11 Lesson 11.6 'Distributive Property and Area'
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 4 Topic D — Distributive Property
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Split Either Factor
learn.content: You can split EITHER number. 6 × 8 = (5+1) × 8 = 5×8 + 1×8 = 40+8 = 48. The distributive property works either way.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.11 Lesson 11.6 'Distributive Property and Area'
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 4 Topic D — Distributive Property
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Apply to Area
learn.content: When a rectangle is too big to recall directly, split it visually into 2 smaller rectangles whose areas you know. Add the areas. This is finding area using the distributive property.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.11 Lesson 11.6 'Distributive Property and Area'
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 4 Topic D — Distributive Property
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Lesson Summary
learn.content: Today: (1) Distributive: a × (b+c) = a×b + a×c. (2) Splits hard facts into easier ones. (3) Visualized as splitting a rectangle. (4) Many valid splits — all give same answer. (5) Useful for area when factors are big.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.11 Lesson 11.6 'Distributive Property and Area'
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 4 Topic D — Distributive Property
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015
